clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Establish baseline CVD risk and inform management decisions in adults with new hypertension diagnosis
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial diagnosis of hypertension, annual follow-up assessments, medication monitoring
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Comprehensive assessment plan including history, physical exam, laboratory testing, and cardiac evaluation

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for hypertension initial assessment
Consider cardiac biomarkers, echocardiography, CAC scoring

validationRules: Diagnosis status required for initial assessment
Annual labs required if on therapy

rules: For new diagnosis → order comprehensive medical history, physical exam, routine laboratory testing
Baseline labs establish CVD risk and inform management decisions
Annual laboratory monitoring for: therapy adverse effects (serum electrolytes), kidney disease progression (UACR, eGFR), CVD risk changes (lipid profile)
ECG to detect subclinical CVD (e.g., left ventricular hypertrophy)
Cardiac biomarkers, echocardiography, CAC scoring for refined risk estimation
Monitor for prevalence and extent of subclinical CVD

laboratoryTests.purpose: monitor changes in predicted CVD risk

cardiacAssessments.purpose: detect subclinical CVD (e.g., left ventricular hypertrophy)
refined risk estimation for CVD, assess subclinical CVD
refined risk estimation for CVD, assess subclinical CVD extent

recommendedLabs.description: List of laboratory tests to order

cardiacTestingRecommended.description: ECG, biomarkers, echocardiography, CAC scoring as indicated

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Initiate antihypertensive therapy based on baseline CVD risk assessment
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Repeat laboratory testing at least annually
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Annual lipid profile, electrolytes, UACR, eGFR
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Alert provider if subclinical CVD detected on ECG or advanced imaging
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log baseline CVD risk, laboratory results, cardiac findings to health record
